description_long: This is a large, stylized wooden mallet with a cylindrical head made from a single log, featuring prominent wood grain texture and growth rings visible on the striking faces. The head is reinforced with two wide, metallic bands, each fastened by four round rivets, providing both structural integrity and a rugged aesthetic. The handle is straight, also wooden, and is encircled by two black metallic rings for added grip and durability. The top of the mallet head has a small, raised cylindrical cap, likely for decorative or reinforcing purposes. The mallet's overall appearance suggests it is designed for heavy-duty tasks, such as driving stakes or demolition, and its exaggerated proportions give it a cartoonish or fantasy-inspired look. The object is clean, with no visible signs of wear, branding, or accessories.
description: A large, stylized wooden mallet with a cylindrical log head, reinforced by metal bands and a straight wooden handle. The mallet features detailed wood grain and metallic accents, giving it a robust and fantasy-inspired appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical wooden head with two metal bands and rivets, and the straight handle below.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the circular end of the mallet head with visible wood rings and the handle extending downward.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the opposite side of the mallet head and handle, with metal bands and rivets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other circular end of the mallet head with wood rings and the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with visible wood grain, reinforced by two black metallic rings, and a matte, slightly textured finish for grip.
attribute_head_type: Large cylindrical wooden head, resembling a sledge or mallet type, with two wide metal bands and round rivets for reinforcement; both ends are flat striking faces with visible wood rings.
